mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the unemployment rate indicate a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Rising unemployment typically precedes recessions and reduces consumer confidence.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE has risen by at least 0.3 percentage points over the prior three months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate falls below a critical threshold, it signals aggressive monetary easing by the central bank to stimulate the economy. This typically supports equity valuations by lowering discount rates and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Central banks cut rates during economic downturns to stimulate growth.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS is below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining retail sales and rising unemployment indicates a severe contraction in consumer demand and purchasing power. This directly translates to lower corporate revenues and profits, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months AND RSAFS declines > 1% in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is a major component of GDP and corporate earnings.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of instances where both specified conditions for UNRATE and RSAFS are met.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a significant decline in industrial production signals a broad-based slowdown in manufacturing and business activity. This directly reduces corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key coincident indicator of economic activity and corporate health.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during identified contraction regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained weakness in housing starts reflects a deteriorating real estate market and broader economic uncertainty. This negatively impacts consumer wealth and confidence, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market health is often a leading indicator for the broader economy and consumer sentiment.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in more than 60% of instances when HOUST is declining on a 3-month annualized basis.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in non-farm payrolls indicates widespread job losses, signaling a severe economic downturn. This reduces consumer income and corporate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 50,000 persons month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Job growth is a critical driver of economic expansion and consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite significant payroll declines.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with slowing retail sales indicates stagflationary pressures, where rising costs erode purchasing power and corporate margins. This leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as economic growth stalls while prices rise.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 4% year-over-year AND RSAFS growth < 0% year-over-year
evidence_refs: fact:Stagflation is historically detrimental to equity markets due to reduced real earnings.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of instances where both high inflation and negative retail sales growth are observed.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, strong GDP growth signals robust corporate earnings and investor confidence. This leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as companies benefit from increased economic activity and higher demand.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong economic growth typically supports equity markets by boosting corporate profits.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during identified expansion regimes.
